hypothesis: If a 3D electron-density encoder is contrastively aligned with a SMILES encoder on paired molecules, then its learned embeddings will be pose-invariant and outperform unimodal and SE(3)-equivariant baselines on unseen-rotation retrieval and property-consistent clustering, because the cross-modal objective forces the 3D branch to discard rotational degrees of freedom not predictive of the SMILES representation.
